mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll expansion raises expected household income and corporate revenue growth, so a positive 3-month change in PAYEMS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 136765 thousand as of November 2013
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 52% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating earnings expectations, causing investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.0 percent as of November 2013
regime_hint: early recession risk or late-cycle labor deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average or if negative-return hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero FEDFUNDS combined with rising INDPRO indicates accommodative policy supporting real activity, compressing discount rates while earnings expectations improve and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent and INDPRO has a positive 3-month change
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.08 percent as of November 2013
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and rising-production condition fails to deliver positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases or underperforms the unconditional 60-day return by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in RSAFS signals stronger consumer demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the data release is absorbed.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month growth crosses above its trailing 12-month median
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 432310 million dollars as of November 2013
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS acceleration triggers do not produce higher average 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations or if the out-of-sample hit rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals slowing construction activity, weaker durable-goods demand, and reduced confidence in cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 883 thousand units as of August 2013
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero signals an inflation squeeze on real rates and future policy risk, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 233.808 as of October 2013
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-pressure and zero-rate interaction has a nonnegative average 20-day forward return or if the negative-return hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving capacity utilization and manufacturing earnings breadth, leading analysts to raise profit expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 99.9778 as of October 2013
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO upside triggers underperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample tests or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real activity breadth is strongest when GDPC1, RSAFS, and PAYEMS rise together, signaling durable income and spending growth that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and both RSAFS and PAYEMS have positive 3-month changes
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 15819.0 billions of chained 2017 dollars as of July 2013
failure_condition: Reject if the joint broad-growth condition produces lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than any single component signal or if positive-return frequency is below 55% out of sample.
